items.title: Schoolboy beaten by group in viral Tawau video sparks probe
items.summary: TAWAU: A video showing a schoolboy being beaten by a group of individuals is under police investigation after the victim lodged a report. Acting Tawau district police chief Superintendent Wilynton Enchana Watt said a report was received at 8.09pm before the Criminal Investigation Division at the Tawau district police headquarters began investigations. "However, one member of the group assaulted and punched the victim before several other students also joined in the attack.

items.title: Anwar defends Pakatan’s Johor push: ‘This is about helping the people’
items.summary: KULAI, July 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim emphasised that the visit by Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) top leadership to Johor is not intended to stir conflict, but rather to put forward policy measures designed to uplift the welfare of the state’s people. “This Johor state election is our way of helping the people. Has the Johor government not helped?

items.title: Anwar turns to Tibetan ginseng to cope with hectic campaign schedule
items.summary: KULAI: Pakatan Harapan chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has revealed his secret to coping with his packed Johor election campaign schedule — Tibetan ginseng. Anwar said the gruelling campaign trail required extra energy as he moved from one programme to another with little time in between. Because I take ginseng — Tibetan ginseng," he said, drawing laughter from the crowd at the Kita GenK Madani X programme.

items.title: MCA seeks probe into alleged campaign material in Johor polls
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: MCA has raised concerns over alleged defamatory campaign material published in a vernacular newspaper during the ongoing Johor election, claiming it amounts to propaganda and a breach of election laws. MCA secretary-general Datuk Chong Sin Woon said the material, published in Sin Chew Daily, contained statements urging voters to reject the party. MCA said the content could fall under Section 11(1)(c) of the Election Offences Act 1954 for improper election publications and campaign conduct.

items.title: Two rallies in Kota Kinabalu split over corruption claims and support for CM Hajiji
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, July 4 — Two separate marches took place in the state capital today, with one led by student activists calling for greater accountability over corruption allegations in Sabah, while another gathered in support of Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor. About 50 people, mostly university students and civil society activists, took part in an anti-corruption march named Gempar Rasuah 3.0, organised by Suara Mahasiswa, while a separate pro-government gathering of about 100 participants moved along a different route through the city before ending at Kota Kinabalu City Park. Protesters carried banners, placards, loudspeakers and megaphones while chanting slogans including “Bangkit, bangkit” (rise up) and “tolak rasuah” (reject corruption), as well as singing songs denouncing alleged corruption among political leaders.

items.title: False 5:1 ratio allegation dismissed, UKM says only 3,917 international undergraduates among 46,151 students
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 4 — Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM) has denied allegations circulating in a social media video claiming that the university has five international students for every local student. In a statement today, UKM described the claim of a 5:1 international-to-local student ratio as completely false. Based on the official figures, international undergraduates account for just eight per cent of the total student population, making the actual ratio approximately one international student for every 12 students - far from the alleged 5:1 ratio.

items.title: Food security drive: DPM Zahid vows 50pc import reduction by 2050, phased cuts already stabilising chicken prices
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, July 4 — The Ministry of Rural and Regional Development (KKDW) aims to reduce the country’s dependence on imported food by 50 per cent by 2050, Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said. The Deputy Prime Minister and Rural and Regional Development Minister said the target was set to address the country’s food import costs, which currently amount to about RM80 billion a year. “Currently, we import food worth about RM80 billion a year.

items.title: Malaysia keeps prawn ban: DOF says 30‑day assessment needed once Thai documents complete
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, July 4 — The Department of Fisheries (DOF) requires at least 30 days to assess documents submitted by the Thai authorities regarding the suspension of import permits (IPs) for five prawn species from that country, provided the information received is complete. “The information is currently being assessed by the technical team. If the information provided is complete, we will require at least 30 days to evaluate every document and piece of information submitted.

items.title: 2 friends killed after motorcycles hit buffalo crossing road in Negri Sembilan
items.summary: JELEBU: Two friends were killed after the motorcycles they were riding struck a buffalo crossing the road at KM11 Jalan Lakai-Simpang Durian at 3am today. Jelebu police chief Superintendent Azizan Said said initial investigations found the crash involved two Yamaha Y15ZR motorcycles ridden by the victims, aged 17 and 19. Azizan said police were investigating the case under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987, Section 289 of the Penal Code and Section 10 of the Minor Offences Act 1955.

items.title: Malaysian caught smuggling 3kg of heroin in tuna tins at Macau airport
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A Malaysian man was detained at Macau International Airport last month for allegedly attempting to smuggle nearly 3kg of heroin worth MOP4.18 million (RM2.09 million) hidden in tuna tins. In a statement, Macau police said the 24-year-old arrived in Macau on a flight from a Southeast Asian country late on June 20. An inspection uncovered 49 boxes containing tinned tuna and packets of biscuits.

items.title: Entertainment tax ‘destroying accessibility’: Groups say Malaysians deserve affordable concerts, theme parks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 4 — Several organisations representing Malaysia’s entertainment, tourism and creative industries are calling for a review of the country’s entertainment tax and urging the government to develop a clear roadmap towards its total abolition. “Today, entertainment, tourism, theme parks, cultural and creative experiences are important contributors to economic growth, employment, tourism receipts, community well-being and national identity. Entertainment, tourism, culture and creative experiences are no longer luxuries.

items.title: Anwar: Early Johor polls no guarantee of bigger BN win
items.summary: KULAI: Pakatan Harapan chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim took a swipe at the Johor government's decision to dissolve the state assembly early, saying an early election was no guarantee of a bigger victory for Barisan Nasional. On the election, Anwar said the contest should focus on policies and improving people's lives rather than political infighting. "Whether PH wins Johor or loses Johor, I remain the prime minister.

items.title: Fahmi: Puad's switch in support shows there's no such thing as fixed deposits
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: No political party, including Barisan Nasional (BN), can currently regard any voter group as a fixed deposit, Pakatan Harapan (PH) communications director Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said. He was responding to a claim by former prime minister Datuk Seri Ismail Sabri Yaakob that non-Malay voters could no longer be considered a fixed deposit for DAP. Fahmi pointed to former Umno supreme council member Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi's recent declaration of support for the PH candidate in the Rengit state seat.

items.title: PH dismisses Ong Kian Ming's forecast of BN landslide in Johor
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: Pakatan Harapan (PH) has dismissed predictions by former Bangi member of parliament Dr Ong Kian Ming that Barisan Nasional (BN) will secure a landslide victory in the Johor state election. PH communications director Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said Ong's forecast was inaccurate and far from reality. Fahmi also rejected claims that Ong's remarks were linked to a delay in PH launching its manifesto, saying the document was unveiled at the right time.

items.title: Chinese metaphysics forecast backs Onn Hafiz in Machap
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: As Johor's election campaign intensifies, a Chinese metaphysics consultancy has entered the fray, predicting caretaker menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi will retain Machap. Kai Wee Consulting Group sparked discussion on social media after publishing a metaphysics-based forecast favouring Onn Hafiz's chances of holding the constituency. Born on March 2, 1979, Onn Hafiz is an Earth Goat in the Chinese zodiac.

items.title: PM Anwar declares war on support letters, says crony loans destroy agencies and entrepreneurs
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 4 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim stressed that the practice of using letters of support, political connections and cronyism in approving financing for entrepreneurs must be stopped. Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said the practice only harms government agencies and causes many businesses to fail. The Prime Minister said the government could not prevent business failures that occurred due to market factors, but would not tolerate the misuse of public funds.

items.title: Rembau police launch K9 hunt after reckless wrong‑way driver crashes, flees into palm oil plantation
items.summary: REMBAU, July 4 — Police are on the hunt for a man who fled after allegedly driving recklessly and against the flow of traffic before colliding with two other vehicles while trying to evade police arrest at Kilometre 242 of the North-South Expressway (NSE) northbound, early this morning. Acting Rembau district police chief DSP Azmi Ali said the incident occurred after the police ordered the 30-year-old driver to stop following a check that found the road tax and insurance for the Volkswagen Passat vehicle had expired. He said, however, the man did not stop and instead accelerated, driving dangerously in a zig-zag manner before going against the flow of traffic.

items.title: Johor election triggers massive economic boom for local businesses, hotels
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Hotels, eateries, caterers, transport operators and small traders reported a surge in business as thousands of politicians, campaign workers and returning voters descend on the state. With 172 candidates contesting 56 state seats, political parties have mobilised thousands of election machinery members from across the country, creating demand that stretches far beyond campaign ceramah and walkabouts. Malaysian Association of Hotels Johor chapter chairman Ivan Teo said occupancy has improved, particularly in Johor Baru and several urban centres, although the impact is uneven across the state.

items.title: Super Typhoon Bavi forces emergency preparations in US Pacific islands
items.summary: SAIPAN, Northern Mariana Islands: People in Guam and the Northern Marianas hunkered down on Saturday as the second "super typhoon" to hit the US Pacific territories since April approached with the equivalent of category-5 hurricane winds. "Current forecast tracks continue to indicate a grim outlook for the Marianas," the US National Weather Service warned. "All residents across Guam and the [Northern Mariana Islands] should plan for and anticipate at least tropical storm conditions."

items.title: Gaza slips from global focus as Middle East war reshapes priorities
items.summary: GAZA CITY: The Gaza war was the spark that touched off years of Middle East conflict culminating in the US-Israeli war with Iran, but as Washington and Teheran wrangle over terms for peace, the devastated territory's fate seems largely out of mind. "Ever since the United States went to war with Iran, the whole world has forgotten Gaza and its tragedy. Hamas's unprecedented Oct 7, 2023 attack on Israel triggered a devastating military response in Gaza, drawing in Teheran-backed allies like Hizbollah in Lebanon and Yemen's Houthi rebels – and eventually Iran itself.

items.title: Sungai Muar holds treasure of unique white oyster ecosystem
items.summary: But beneath its murky depths lies a prized gastronomic treasure — white oysters, or Crassostrea (Magallana) saidii. With the guidance of Parit Tiram village head Mohammad Daniel Azahari, 33, affectionately known as Tok Daniel, the writer was shown how the river's oysters are regarded as unique. "Muar oysters are among the best in the world because they are rare white oysters.

items.title: China-Taiwan tensions rise after new coast guard patrol launch
items.summary: BEIJING: China said on Saturday it had launched a new coast guard patrol east of Taiwan, drawing a sharp response from Taipei after a task force last month off the island's coast caused alarm in some Western capitals. China's military operates almost daily around Taiwan, which Beijing views as its own territory. The coast guard will "firmly safeguard China's territorial sovereignty and maritime rights and interests", it added.

items.title: Timber lorry driver arrested after testing positive for meth, ganja
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A timber lorry driver was among two people detained after testing positive for drugs during a screening operation at the Gombak Toll Plaza today. Kuala Lumpur Road Transport Department (RTD) director Hamidi Adam said the 31-year-old driver tested positive for methamphetamine and ganja. Hamidi said enforcement action was taken against 43 vehicles for various offences under the Road Transport Act.

items.title: Boat capsize: Always wear life jackets at sea, says Terengganu exco
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU: The Terengganu government has urged all participants in squid jigging (candat sotong) activities to wear life jackets at all times while on board boats, and advised skippers to conduct safety briefings before departure to reduce the risk of accidents at sea. State Tourism, Culture, Environment and Climate Change Committee chairman Datuk Razali Idris said the wearing of life jackets should not be neglected, even when participants are resting or sleeping on board, as safety must remain the top priority throughout their time at sea. Whether they are crew members, passengers, or squid jigging participants, everyone must wear a life jacket at all times, including while resting or sleeping on board.

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain forecast in parts of eight states until 6pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 4 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in parts of eight states, with the weather expected to persist until 6pm today, according to Bernama. The national news agency reported that the warning covers Kota Setar, Yan, Pendang, Kuala Muda and Sik in Kedah; Manjung and Perak Tengah in Perak; and Kemaman in Terengganu. In Sarawak, the affected areas are Sibu in Sarikei division, and Tanjung Manis and Daro in Mukah division.

items.title: Govt targets 100pct internet access in populated areas
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: The government through the Communications Ministry is targeting 100 per cent internet access in populated areas nationwide by next year through the implementation of the National Digital Network (Jendela). Communications Ministry secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah said that as of Dec 31, broadband coverage in such areas had reached 99.71 per cent, which reflected the high commitment of the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC). Halim said the expansion of good internet access can launch various sectors including education and the economy, especially in rural and interior areas.

items.title: Houthis warn of strikes on Saudi airports over Iran flight
items.summary: SANAA: Yemen's Houthis said on Friday its forces confronted Saudi "warplanes" that they said attempted to prevent an Iranian civilian aircraft from landing at Sanaa International Airport, according to a statement by the group's military spokesperson Yahya Sarea. The Iran-aligned group warned that they would target "Saudi airports and vital interests on land and sea" if the Saudis continue what Sarea called violations of Yemeni airspace. Saudi Arabia led a military coalition that intervened in Yemen in 2015 after the Iran-aligned Houthis seized the capital, Sanaa.

items.title: DBKL announces long-term road closures around Jalan U Thant from Monday
items.summary: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL), in a Facebook post, said the closure will affect the exit from Jalan U Thant to Jalan Tun Razak near Regal House, as well as the entry from Jalan Tun Razak to Jalan Langgak Golf near the Embassy of Iraq. According to DBKL, motorists travelling from Jalan Tun Razak to Jalan U Thant, Jalan Taman U Thant, Jalan Maidge, Persiaran Ampang Hilir, and Jalan Langgak Golf must use Jalan U Thant as the designated entry route. Meanwhile, motorists travelling from Jalan U Thant, Jalan Taman U Thant, Jalan Maidge, Persiaran Ampang Hilir, and Jalan Langgak Golf towards Jalan Tun Razak are required to exit via Jalan Langgak Golf.

items.title: UKM denies claims that international students outnumber locals 5 to 1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM) has dismissed claims that its international students outnumber local students by five to one, saying foreign undergraduates make up eight per cent of its total student population. It said the actual ratio of international to local students is about 1:12, not the 5:1 claimed in a Facebook video. The university said international student enrolment is controlled and aligns with the Higher Education Ministry's agenda to internationalise Malaysia's higher education sector.

items.title: Johor Jaya's Pakatan debutant bets on youth vote to shape state’s future
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 4 — Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) Johor Jaya candidate Lee Wern Yiing believes young voters will play a decisive role in shaping Johor’s future at the upcoming state election. Lee, who is also Johor DAP Socialist Youth (DAPSY) chief, is no stranger to the constituency, having spent the past eight years handling cases in Johor Jaya. The Johor Jaya state seat, located in Johor Bahru, is a growing constituency comprising residential neighbourhoods and light industrial areas.

items.title: Kangar local council seizes 400 units of liquid vape and 70 devices worth RM15,000
items.summary: KANGAR: The Kangar Municipal Council seized 400 units of electronic cigarettes or vape liquid and 70 related devices, worth RM15,000, in an operation at a premises in Jalan Padang Behor here last night. Its president, Affendi Rajini Kanth said the seizure, during the operation between 10.30pm and 1 am, in collaboration with the police, was carried out in accordance with the provisions of the MPK Trade, Business, and Industry Licensing By-Laws, in line with the Perlis state government's proclamation prohibiting the operation of vape sales premises in the state effective Aug 1, 2025. "The premises in question has also been fined several times and a business cessation notice was issued in August 2025, yet it continues to operate the vape business," he said in a statement today.

items.title: Malaysia aims to halve food imports by 2050
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: The government aims to reduce Malaysia's dependence on imported food by up to 50 per cent by 2050 in a bid to curb food imports, which are currently estimated at about RM80 billion annually. He said the effort would be driven by various food security initiatives implemented by the Rural and Regional Development Ministry and its agencies over the past three years. "Our goal is to help the country reduce food imports by 50 per cent from the current level.

items.title: Malaysia on track for 100pc internet coverage in populated areas by 2027
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, July 4 — The government through the Ministry of Communications is targeting 100 per cent Internet access in populated areas nationwide by next year through the implementation of the National Digital Network (JENDELA). Ministry of Communications secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah said that as of Dec 31, broadband coverage in such areas had reached 99.71 per cent, which reflected the high commitment of the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC). He said that before the implementation of Jendela, internet access nationwide was only around 90 per cent, but through the latest phase of the plan, MCMC will focus on Internet access up to 4G and 5G.

items.title: Import ban on 5 Thai shrimp species stays for now
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The import ban on five aquaculture shrimp species from Thailand will remain pending the Fisheries Department's evaluation of data from Thai authorities. On June 1, Malaysia put a temporary ban on five shrimp species from Thailand. The temporary ban involves Penaeus esculentus (brown tiger prawn), Fenneropenaeus merguiensis (banana prawn), Penaeus vannamei (whiteleg shrimp), Penaeus monodon (giant tiger prawn), and Penaeus stylirostris (blue shrimp).

items.title: Anwar says Bumiputera empowerment is national priority, not just agencies’ responsibility
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 4 — The Bumiputera empowerment agenda is no longer the sole responsibility of specific agencies but a national priority to be pursued by all ministries, government agencies and government-linked companies, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. He said government agencies must align their policies and programmes to ensure the Bumiputera development agenda is implemented comprehensively. According to Anwar, the government has introduced the Bumiputera Economic Transformation Plan 2035 (PuTERA35), with implementation regularly monitored and all ministries and agencies required to report their progress.

items.title: BN-Pakatan cooperation remains intact despite Johor campaign heat, says Zahid
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, July 4 — Barisan Nasional (BN) enjoys a very good relationship with Pakatan Harapan (PH) at the federal level, with cooperation between the two political coalitions remaining strong despite the heat of the Johor state election campaign. “Cooperation between BN and PH is very good. Commenting on the current Johor state election campaign, Ahmad Zahid said both political coalitions remain committed to preserving harmony within the Unity Government by adopting a mature approach to election campaigning.

items.title: Johor polls: BN-PH enjoy very good ties at federal level, says Zahid
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI: Barisan Nasional (BN) enjoys a very good relationship with Pakatan Harapan (PH) at the federal level, with cooperation between the two political coalitions remaining strong despite the heat of the Johor state election campaign. BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the leadership of both BN and PH has consistently demonstrated professionalism and political maturity, placing the national agenda above all other considerations. Commenting on the current Johor state election campaign, Zahid said both political coalitions remain committed to preserving harmony within the unity government by adopting a mature approach to election campaigning.

items.title: Johor polls: Onn Abu Bakar outlines 6 pledges for Senggarang's development
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Senggarang state seat, Onn Abu Bakar, today unveiled his 'Six Pledges for a More Progressive and Prosperous Senggarang' manifesto for the upcoming 16th Johor state election. Onn said the first pledge focuses on providing quality internet access for all by advocating improved telecommunications coverage in housing estates, villages and rural areas to support education, business and daily activities. N24: SENGGARANG : 38,576 voters 1.

items.title: Armed Forces to probe after 'distasteful' videos by 'soldier' go viral
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Armed Forces will investigate claims that a "soldier" has been uploading videos on social media accompanied by derogatory captions. Armed Forces chief General Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman said the military was aware of the posts, which have gone viral on Threads. The post drew reactions from netizens, with many saying such actions could tarnish the image and reputation of the Malaysian Armed Forces.

items.title: Stick insects becoming harder to find amid concerns over habitat change
items.summary: "From a scientific perspective, the data does not show that stick insects are currently at risk of extinction. He said Malaysia is home to more than 100 species of stick insects, varying in shape and size, with some resembling twigs while others resembling leaf insects, adding that the insects are mainly distinguished by their long, thin, and tapering bodies, which closely resemble real twigs. He said stick insects are neither poisonous nor venomous, although some species have spines on their bodies for self-defence.

items.title: Lee: Selangor MB's Johor investment comparison oversimplifies economic realities
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A caretaker Johor executive councillor has defended the state's economic performance after Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari claimed its investment inflows failed to generate enough local jobs. Johor caretaker Investment, Trade, Consumer Affairs and Human Resources Committee chairman Lee Ting Han said data from federal agencies proved Johor's economic foundation remained solid. Amirudin had said Selangor attracted RM83 billion in investments last year, creating 60,000 new jobs, compared to around 40,000 jobs created in Johor.

items.title: [UPDATED] PM: PUNB targets RM2.25bil financing, govt may boost support
items.summary: The agency also unveiled three new financing programmes, namely, Prosper Grow Biz Express, Prosper Grow Fuel Up and Prosper Grow Auto Biz, aimed at improving access to financing and strengthening entrepreneurs' working capital. The RM2.25 billion financing target will be achieved through PUNB's existing facilities — Prosper Grow, Prosper Great and Prosper Impact/Nova — alongside enhancements to programmes tailored to meet the evolving needs of entrepreneurs. Held ahead of PUNB's 35th anniversary, SParK 2026 brought together PUNB Entrepreneur Partners, Bumiputera entrepreneurs, corporate leaders, industry players, digital ecosystem participants, development agencies and strategic partners for conferences, knowledge-sharing sessions, business exhibitions and sales activities.

items.title: Government’s RM429m allocation since 2023 ensures Johor’s enforcement agencies thrive
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 4 — The Home Ministry (KDN) has allocated more than RM429 million since 2023 to strengthen the welfare of personnel and enhance the operational capabilities of enforcement agencies in Johor. Its minister, Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail, said the allocation covers three key agencies, namely the Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM), the Malaysian Immigration Department (JIM), and the Malaysian Prisons Department. Saifuddin Nasution said the allocation comprised RM174.8 million for projects that have been completed or are currently under implementation, and RM255 million for projects in the planning stage.

items.title: Johor campaign opens quietly as parties shift to targeted voter outreach
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The first week of campaigning in the 16th Johor state election has seen relatively moderate momentum, with all parties opting to engage voters more targeted rather than holding large-scale rallies. Political analyst Prof Datuk Dr Sivamurugan Pandian said all parties are adapting their campaign methods to the current political environment through more personal approaches, including house visits, small group meetings and community programmes. He said such strategies not only give candidates the opportunity to meet voters face-to-face and understand their concerns, but also help parties strengthen their machinery and make better use of campaign resources ahead of a more critical second phase.

items.title: Tonton names 223 winners in first round of 'Non Stop, On Top!' campaign
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Tonton has announced 223 winners in the first round of its 'Tonton Non Stop, On Top!' The grand prize winner will be announced during a prize presentation ceremony on July 7. Following the conclusion of the first round, the second round of the campaign began on June 29 and will run until July 12.

items.title: Puad Zarkashi declares support for Pakatan’s Rengit hopeful, says choice based on personality
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 4 — Former Umno supreme council member Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi has today declared his support for Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) Rengit candidate Mohamad Yazid Bakri for the Johor state election. The former Johor state assembly speaker and Rengit assemblyman had openly expressed his support for PH’s Mohamad Yazid through a campaign poster. In the 2022 Johor state election, Puad won the Rengit state seat with a 1,920 majority vote.

items.title: Mohamad Sabu leads Malaysian delegation in Tehran, pays final respects to slain Iranian leader Khamenei
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 4 — Minister of Agriculture and Food Security Datuk Seri Mohamad Sabu led the Malaysian government delegation in paying final respects to the late former Supreme Leader of Iran, Ali Khamenei, at the Grand Mosalla in Tehran. In a Facebook post today, Mohamad said the mourning ceremonies would take place over several days, beginning in Tehran before continuing to Qom, Najaf, and Karbala, prior to the remains being taken to the Imam Reza Shrine in Mashhad for burial on July 9. “Alhamdulillah, the final respect for the late former Supreme Leader of Iran, Ali Khamenei, at the Grand Mosalla has just concluded.

items.title: Anwar says Johor needs smarter, bolder representatives, pledges Harapan will defend people’s interests
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 4 — The people of Johor need leaders and elected representatives who are wise in assessing priorities, have the courage to make decisions and truly understand the pulse of the people, said Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said the coalition would continue to carry the people’s voice, defend the state’s interests and drive Johor to become more developed and great for all. “Johor Progresses.

items.title: Penang DOSH issues prohibition notice after worker struck, killed by falling scaffolding platform
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, July 4 — The Department of Occupational Safety and Health (DOSH) Penang branch has issued a prohibition notice to halt work immediately at a Bandar Tanjong Pinang construction site following a fatal accident on July 1. In a statement, Penang DOSH said the order was issued as investigations are ongoing into the 1.30pm incident involving an Indonesian construction worker who was confirmed dead after being struck by a scaffolding working platform that fell from the eighth floor of a building. “Following the incident, a prohibition notice was issued to stop related work activities, along with a non-disturbance order to preserve the scene in its original condition until investigations are completed,” read the statement.

items.title: Amirudin dismisses claims Pakatan manifesto copied, says affordable housing and healthcare plans original
items.summary: KLUANG, July 4 — PKR vice-president Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari has dismissed claims that Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) manifesto for the 16th Johor State Election was copied from other parties, saying it was developed by the coalition’s leadership over several months. He said initiatives such as affordable housing and healthcare assistance were the result of careful planning by the PH leadership. Also present were PKR vice-president R. Ramanan, Amanah secretary-general Faiz Fadzil, PH candidate for the Machap state seat Nur Hafiz Roslan, PH Benut candidate Abd Razak Ismail and PH Layang-Layang candidate Guna Balakrishnan.

items.title: PM Anwar launches SParK 2026, PUNB sets RM2.25b financing target to drive Bumiputera growth
items.summary: The agency has announced a financing approval target of up to RM2.25 billion to strengthen the development of Bumiputera entrepreneurs for 2026–2030. Anwar said the national agency, established to develop Bumiputera entrepreneurs, has performed well thus far. It also introduced three programmes — Prosper Grow Biz Express, Prosper Grow Fuel Up, and Prosper Grow Auto Biz — to improve financing access and strengthen entrepreneurs’ working capital.

items.title: Bukit Aman flags 50pc jump in reckless driving cases, warns surge could trigger road rage accidents
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 4 — Cases of reckless and dangerous driving have increased by nearly 50 per cent in the first six months of this year compared to the same period last year. The increase reflects a worrying trend of aggressive driving, which could lead to road bullying or road rage incidents. Bukit Aman Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department (JSPT) director Datuk Seri Muhammed Hasbullah Ali said 1,278 investigation papers were opened from Jan 1 to June 28 this year, compared to 852 cases in the same period last year, an increase of 426 cases.

items.title: College worker with prior child sex convictions arrested in Shah Alam after teenage boys allege assaults
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 4 — Police have arrested a man suspected of sexually assaulting two 15‑year‑old male college students in separate incidents here. According to Bernama, Shah Alam police chief ACP Sarudin Samah said the suspect, a local man in his 30s with a previous criminal record involving child sexual offences, was picked up at a restaurant in Shah Alam at 12.01am today. “The first victim alleged he was molested in the restroom of the college dormitory, while the second claimed he was sodomised at the suspect’s house in Section 18, Shah Alam,” Sarudin said in a statement to Bernama.

items.title: State elections are not referendums: Johor, Negeri Sembilan and the limits of local ballots — Phar Kim Beng
items.summary: State elections are important in their own right, but they are not, and cannot be, binding judgements on the federal government’s performance in national security, foreign policy, defence, macroeconomic governance, or the constitutional integrity of Malaysia – including Borneo. Different mandates, different responsibilities Malaysia’s federal architecture draws a clear line between what Putrajaya controls and what state capitals do. The federal government holds the levers that determine national security policy, defence planning, foreign relations, national taxation and redistribution, as well as the broad contours of macroeconomic policy.

items.title: Election Commission records 305 Johor campaign offence cases, most linked to illegal posters
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 4 — The Election Commission (EC) has received 305 reports and complaints involving various campaign offences during the 16th Johor State Election campaign period as of July 2. It said 27 cases involved campaign materials placed within a 50-metre radius of polling centres, while the remaining 48 involved other offences. A total of 56 Election Campaign Enforcement Teams (PP-KPR) were established to enforce the Election Offences Act 1954 throughout the campaign period, which runs from nomination day on June 27 until July 10.

items.title: No casualties as Shah Alam PKNS Complex fire contained, operations resume next day
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, July 4 — The Selangor State Development Corporation (PKNS) has confirmed that the Shah Alam PKNS Complex resumed normal operations yesterday after all required procedures and safety measures were met following a fire at the premises Thursday afternoon. PKNS also said the Selangor Fire and Rescue Department managed to bring the fire under control within a short period, preventing it from spreading to other parts of the building. The Fire Department is still investigating the exact cause of the fire, it added.

items.title: Fatal Bera collision claims life of anti‑drugs officer, MPV driver detained after Erimin test
items.summary: Bera police chief Supt Zulkiflee Nazir said Mohamed Sharif Mohamed Ali, 37, who was serving in Bera, died at the scene of the collision, which occurred at about 10am. “The MPV driver is believed to have lost control of his vehicle, which veered onto the opposite lane before colliding with the car driven by Mohamed Sharif, causing his car to skid onto the roadside shoulder,” he said in a statement. Zulkiflee said the MPV driver and passenger suffered minor injuries to their legs.

items.title: Malaysians still visiting Sungai Golok after Tak Bai blast, but avoiding longer trips
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 4 — Malaysians are continuing to visit Sungai Golok in southern Thailand following Monday’s bomb blast in Tak Bai, although many are choosing to limit their trips to the border town over security concerns, according to Sinar Harian. “So far, we still dare to come to Sungai Golok town and feel safe. “Today we’re just eating in Sungai Golok town.

items.title: Wee dismisses claims of MCA-PN collaboration in Johor polls as ‘made-up stories’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 4 — MCA president Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong has dismissed allegations that his party is working with Perikatan Nasional (PN) in the Johor state election, describing the claims as baseless attempts to tarnish Barisan Nasional’s (BN) image, according to Free Malaysia Today (FMT). According to FMT, Johor DAP had accused BN and PN of cooperating after PAS deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man urged PAS members and supporters to vote for Umno or BN candidates in constituencies where PN is not contesting. Wee also rejected claims that a landslide BN victory in the Johor polls would pave the way for former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak’s release.

items.title: Squid jigging trip turns tragic after boat capsizes off Kuala Terengganu, leaving one dead
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, July 4 — One person died, three were injured and eight others were rescued after a squid jigging boat capsized about nine nautical miles off Dataran Kuala Nerus last night. Kuala Terengganu police chief ACP Azli Mohd Noor said in the 9pm incident, the deceased was crew member Ahmad Nasaruddin Mohmad Jalil, 37, while three participants were injured, namely Faris Ibrahim, 33; Ahmad Danial Iman, 24; and Fatin Fariesya Rohaizan, 26. Boat operator Mohd Awais Sa’ari, 39, and seven other participants survived the incident.
